what: anagotus.data: Label data for Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections
title: anagotus.data: Label data for Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections
text: Label data Anagotus spp. specimens located museum collections. Anagotus genus large, flightless weevils (Coleoptera: Curculionidae) endemic New Zealand. package provides dataset label data Anagotus specimens housed museum collections New Zealand overseas, including temporal spatial information available.

title: anagotus.data: Label data for Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections
anagotus.data: Label data for Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections
text: dataset contains 2,145 specimen records 15 variables including institution codes, collection dates, coordinates, locality descriptions, species identifications. See ?df_anagotus full documentation variables.

title: anagotus.data: Label data for Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections
text: Specimen label data digitised Neil Birrell museum collections New Zealand overseas. Terrestrial Freshwater Biodiversity Information System (TFBIS) managed Manaaki Whenua – Landcare Research, New Zealand.

title: anagotus.data: Label data for Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections
anagotus.data: Anagotus Label Data — anagotus.data-package
text: package provides label data Anagotus specimens stored various institutions. includes temporal spatial data possible.

title: Label data of Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections — df_anagotus
Label data of Anagotus spp. specimens located in museum collections — df_anagotus
text: tibble 2,145 rows 15 variables: institution_code chr Code institution housing specimen. Codes standardised : NZAC = New Zealand Arthropod Collection, New Zealand; MONZ = Museum New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, New Zealand; AK = Auckland Museum, New Zealand; CMNZ = Canterbury Museum, New Zealand; OMNZ = Otago Museum, New Zealand; NHMUK = Natural History Museum, London, United Kingdom; LUNZ = Lincoln University Collection, New Zealand; ANIC = Australian National Insect Collection, Canberra, Australia; FRNZ = Forest Research Institute (now BSI - Scion), New Zealand. catalog_number chr unique barcode identifier specimen. corresponds physical label specimen, , case tfbis dataset, unique code identifying information digitised (NUMBCYYMM, C=century, YY = year, MM = month, NUMB = number). recorded_by chr collector specimen, known. year num year specimen collected, known. month num month specimen collected, known. decimal_latitude num latitude decimal degrees specimen collected. often estimated place name. decimal_longitude num longitude decimal degrees specimen collected. often estimated place name. location chr textual description location specimen collected. species chr latin binomial tag name specimen. cases, specific epithet indicated *, manuscript names Willy Kuschel used formal publications without checking taxonomic expert. generic_name chr genus name specimen. specific_name chr species name specimen. cases, specific epithet indicated *, manuscript names Willy Kuschel used formal publications without checking taxonomic expert. microhabitat chr habitat microhabitat associated specimen, per label data. data_origin chr data sourced. values : nwb_<institution_code>digitised = data digitised Neil Birrell physical specimen labels museum collections; curator_database_checked = data downloaded museum collection databases museum curators. case CMNZ undergoing renovations time physical visit possible. tfbis<institution_code>digitised = data downloaded Terrestrial Freshwater Biodiversity Information System (TFBIS) managed Manaaki Whenua - Landcare Research, New Zealand. includes variety collections. case NZAC may contain duplicates digitised Neil Birrell. unique identifier attached specimens tfbis data, possible cross check duplicates. important , use nwb<institution_code>_digitised values comprehensive checks one researcher. df_origin chr dataframe value originated . correspond particular csv can found data-raw folder. day int day specimen collected, known.
